summary:
Israel has established a covert base in Iraq for special forces and search-and-rescue teams to conduct operations against Iran. Israeli forces launched airstrikes on Iraqi troops who nearly discovered the site early in the war. The base affects Iraqi sovereignty. Israeli forces continue to defend it amid the ongoing conflict with Iran.

Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since U.S. and Iranian officials reported severe injuries from an airstrike two months ago. Negotiators lack his guidance on the U.S. peace proposal to end the two-month war. Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned of targeting U.S. sites in the Middle East if their tankers face attacks, as the U.S. awaits Tehran's reply. The Strait of Hormuz experienced relative calm after recent flare-ups, with no movement yet from Tehran on formally ending fighting; global oil flows through the strait previously faced disruptions from affected seafarers and tankers. In Lebanon, Israeli strikes killed 39 people despite a ceasefire announcement last month with Hezbollah; exchanges of fire continue near the border, affecting Lebanese civilians and fighters.

Jihadist groups killed dozens in assaults on villages in central Mali on Saturday, raising the toll over recent days to more than 70; local residents and security forces suffered losses, with governments preparing responses. A car bomb at a checkpoint in Pakistan killed 12 people, including security personnel and civilians; investigations into perpetrators continue. A cruise ship with a hantavirus outbreak docked in Spain's Canary Islands after three deaths and multiple illnesses; Spanish authorities started controlled evacuation of hundreds of high-risk passengers, followed by quarantines and contact tracing. Vietnam entered advanced talks for a US$700 million purchase of BrahMos supersonic missiles from India during President To Lam’s visit; 15 other nations have expressed interest amid South China Sea tensions.

headlines.title: Israel Builds and Defends Secret Base in Iraq for Special Forces Against Iran
headlines.summary: Israel establishes a covert base in Iraq for special forces and search-and-rescue teams, launching airstrikes on Iraqi troops who nearly discovered it early in the war. This escalates regional tensions, affects Iraqi sovereignty, and reveals extent of Israeli operations. Next steps involve ongoing defense amid Iran conflict.

headlines.title: Iranian Supreme Leader Remains Absent Publicly Since Airstrike Injury Two Months Ago
headlines.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since U.S. and Iranian officials report he suffered severe injuries in an airstrike over two months ago. This absence hampers negotiators seeking his guidance on U.S. peace proposals. It matters as it complicates ending the two-month war.

headlines.title: Jihadist Fighters Kill Dozens in Wave of Attacks Across Central Mali
headlines.summary: Jihadist groups launch deadly assaults on villages in central Mali, killing dozens on Saturday and raising the toll to over 70 in recent days. Local residents and security forces are affected. Intensifying attacks threaten regional stability; governments prepare responses.

headlines.title: Car Bomb Kills 12 People at Checkpoint in Pakistan
headlines.summary: A car bomb detonates at a checkpoint in Pakistan, reducing the structure to rubble and killing 12. Security personnel and nearby civilians suffer losses. Incident underscores ongoing militant threats; investigations into perpetrators continue, impacting counterterrorism efforts.

headlines.title: Hantavirus-Afflicted Cruise Ship Arrives in Spain's Canary Islands for Evacuation
headlines.summary: Cruise ship with hantavirus outbreak docks in Canary Islands after three deaths and multiple illnesses. Hundreds of high-risk passengers face controlled evacuation by Spanish authorities. Outbreak highlights global health risks from travel; quarantines and tracing follow.

headlines.title: Iran Revolutionary Guards Threaten U.S. Sites Over Potential Tanker Attacks
headlines.summary: Iran’s Guards warn of targeting U.S. sites in Middle East if their tankers face fire, as U.S. awaits Tehran’s reply to peace proposal. Regional military assets and shipping affected. Escalates tensions in ongoing war; response could trigger wider conflict.

headlines.title: Vietnam Enters Advanced Talks for US$700 Million BrahMos Missiles from India
headlines.summary: Vietnam negotiates supersonic BrahMos missile purchase from India during President To Lam’s visit, joining 15 others interested. Asian nations affected by shift from Western arms. Deal signals regional arms race amid South China Sea tensions; boosts India’s exports.

headlines.title: Lebanon Reports Israeli Strikes Kill 39 Despite Ceasefire Announcement
headlines.summary: Israeli strikes kill 39 in Lebanon as Israel and Hezbollah continue fire exchanges despite ceasefire last month. Lebanese civilians and fighters affected near border. Ongoing violations risk broader war; monitoring for compliance needed.
headlines.previousContext: Israeli strikes kill 39 amid continued trading of fire despite ceasefire deal announced last month

headlines.title: Strait of Hormuz Sees Relative Calm as U.S. Awaits Iran Peace Response
headlines.summary: Calm returns to Strait of Hormuz after flare-ups, with U.S. expecting Iran’s reply to war-ending proposal within hours. Seafarers and tankers affected previously. Matters for global oil flows; no movement yet from Tehran on formal end to fighting.
headlines.previousContext: Sporadic flare-ups subside into relative calm after seafarers stranded dodging drones
